calendar.matters:
DEFENDANT AALAEI ALI AND NILOFAR TRUST'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT (ADDED PER THE COURT'S 4/21/26 RULING)
Off Calendar Apr-27-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT AALAEI ALI AND NILOFAR TRUST'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT

Appearances via Zoom:
David Ericksen of Ericksen Legal (415.652.4031) for plaintiff and opposing party;
Lee Ackerman of the Law Office of Lee B. Ackerman (310.990.2290) for defendant and moving party.

Argued and the court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Defendant Aalaei Ali and Nilofar Trust's motion to strike the complaint is granted without leave to amend and this case is dismissed. All six causes of action alleged by plaintiffs are related to the claims alleged in the case 23CV425115 pending in Santa Clara Superior Court and are therefore compulsory cross-claims which were required by CCP 426.30(a) to be filed in 23CV425115 rather than as a separate lawsuit in this court. (Currie Medical Specialties, Inc. v. Bowen (1982) 136 Cal.App.3d 774, 777 ("The waiver provision of section 426.30 is mandatory, the policy in favor of hearing all related claims in a single action controlling.")) The fact that there are different parties in this case than in 23CV425115 does not make section 426.30 inapplicable. Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order. Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Reporter: Jane Hong, CSR 11975 (909.831.2644, CSRJane@hotmail.com)

Amended Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Order Granting Motion To Strike Pursuant To Ccp 473 And (Proposed] Order
Off Calendar Apr-21-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFFS MUSEUM PLAZA, LLC, WALID MANDO, AND FORMA, LLC'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE ORDER GRANTING MOTION TO STRIKE PURSUANT TO CCP SEC. 473

No appearances. The court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Plaintiffs' motion to set aside the February 3, 2026 order striking the complaint without leave to amend is granted. By opposing the motion on the merits, defendants waived any defects in the filing and service of the motion papers. (Carlton v. Quint (2000) 77 Cal. App. 4th 690). David Ericksen's declaration establishes plaintiffs' entitlement to the vacation of the February 3, 2026 order. The motion to strike will be heard on April 27, 2026. Plaintiffs must file their opposition papers to the motion to strike no later than April 21, 2026. Defendants may file any reply papers on their motion to strike no later than April 23, 2026. Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order. Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (301/HEK)

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-03-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT AALAEI ALI AND NILOFAR TRUST's MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT.

The Motion to Strike Complaint is granted without leave to amend, and the matter is dismissed. (Carroll v. Import Motors, Inc. (1995) 33 Cal.App.4th 1429, 1434-1437.) The Court strikes the Complaint filed in this action on January 23, 2025. The Complaint is barred by Code of Civil Procedure section 426.30(a), which provides, "Except as otherwise provided by statute, if a party against whom a complaint has been filed and served fails to allege in a cross-complaint any related cause of action which (at the time of serving his answer to the complaint) he has against the plaintiff, such party may not thereafter in any other action assert against the plaintiff the related cause of action not pleaded." The Complaint should have been filed as a Cross-Complaint in the Santa Clara County Superior Court, in Case No. 23CV425115. The Court finds that the Complaint in this action arises from the same transaction that served as the basis for the Santa Clara County Superior Court case. (See Currie Medical Specialties, Inc. v. Bowen (1982) 136 Cal.App.3d 774, 777 ("The waiver provision of section 426.30 is mandatory, the policy in favor of hearing all related claims in a single action controlling.")

Proposed order submitted.

Judge: Christopher C. Hite; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (301/CCH)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Sanctions Against Plaintiffs Museum Plaza, Llc, Walid Mando, Forma, Llc And Their Attorney Of Record David Allen Ericksen Pursuant To California Code Of Civil Procedure Sections 128.5 And 128.7; Declaration Of Ali Aalaei
Off Calendar Jan-16-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT ARI LAW, P.C.'S Motion For Sanctions Against Plaintiffs Museum Plaza, LLC, Walid Mando, Forma, LLC And Their Attorney Of Record David Allen Ericksen Pursuant To California Code Of Civil Procedure Sections 128.5 And 128.7.

Defendant Ari Law, P.C.'s motion for CCP 128.5 and 128.7 sanctions is denied. Ari Law has not shown that the filing of this lawsuit was made in bad faith, is frivolous, solely intended to cause unnecessary delay or was done for an improper purpose. While there are significant issues that will likely be addressed at a later time regarding the propriety of the filing of this lawsuit, because the application of exclusive concurrent jurisdiction is arguably a discretionary call based on policy considerations and not a hard and fast rule (see Shaw v. Superior Court (2022) 78 Cal. App. 5th 245, 256-257), there is a nonfrivolous argument allowing for the filing of this lawsuit.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to deliver a hard copy of a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling delivered directly to Department 301.

Visiting Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (301/HEK)

MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
Off Calendar Jan-16-2026 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT ARI LAW, P.C. A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION, ALI AALAEI MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS

Defendants Ari Law, P.C. and Ali Ari Aalaei's motion to quash is denied. Because the doctrine of exclusive concurrent jurisdiction is not jurisdictional in the sense a court lacks the authority to entertain a case (see Shaw v. Superior Court (2922) 78 Cal. App. 5th 245, 258), a motion to quash is not the proper procedural vehicle to raise that doctrine. Instead, the doctrine must be raised by a demurrer or motion to stay. (Id. at 256, n. 5).

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to deliver a hard copy of a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling directly to Department 301.

Visiting Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (301/HEK)

Because the doctrine of exclusive concurrent jurisdiction is not jurisdictional in the sense a court lacks the authority to entertain a case (see Shaw v. Superior Court (2922) 78 Cal. App. 5th 245, 258), a motion to quash is not the proper procedural vehicle to raise that doctrine. Instead, the doctrine must be raised by a demurrer or motion to stay. (Id. at 256, n. 5).

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to deliver a hard copy of a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling directly to Department 301.
